title: spinacia oleracea var. inermis
intro: Spinacia oleracea var. inermis, also known as wild spinach or prickly spinach, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Spinacia oleracea var. inermis has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant's leaves can be prepared as a herbal infusion, often in combination with other herbs, or used fresh in salads and cooking.
